capabilities: Apply hierarchical task decomposition in agent planning by leveraging software design patterns to enhance task specialization and efficiency.

procedure.action: Compose software design patterns into task decomposition by mapping each task to a pattern that optimizes execution; use pattern matching algorithms to ensure correct mapping by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Execute task decomposition using a domain-specific planning tool, ensuring each subtask adheres to the constraints defined by the selected design pattern.
Observe execution by logging operation_duration_ms and result_code for each task; ensure error_count < 1 and result_valid = true for successful execution.

Assert the output of each decomposed task by running domain-specific validation scripts; assert that all outputs meet the predefined success criteria by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Log all task execution details, including operation_duration_ms and result_code, to an audit trail for traceability and future analysis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Specialize hierarchical task decomposition in agent planning by integrating software design patterns, ensuring domain-specific constraints are met with measurable outcomes.
title: Hierarchical Task Decomposition for Agent Planning

failureConditions: Task execution fails when design patterns are incorrectly mapped, causing inefficiencies and increased error rates.
Inconsistent task outputs when domain-specific constraints are not enforced, leading to invalid results and potential system failures.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
the output of each decomposed task by running domain-specific validation scripts
that all outputs meet the predefined success criteria by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition
